  ],
  "text": "The Grafana backend plugin management system is implemented across multiple packages in pkg/plugins/. Here is how the core components work together:\n\n**Process Supervisor and Restart Policy on Crash:**\nThe `pkg/plugins/manager/process/process.go` file contains the `Service` struct which acts as the process supervisor. The `Start()` method (line 22) initializes a backend plugin process and calls `startPluginAndKeepItAlive()` (line 48) which spawns a goroutine running `keepPluginAlive()` (line 67). The `keepPluginAlive()` function implements a ticker-based supervisor that continuously monitors the plugin process. If the plugin exits (`p.Exited()` returns true, line 77), it automatically restarts the plugin by calling `p.Start()` with a `context.Background()` (line 82). This restart policy ensures plugins are automatically recovered from crashes. The `Stop()` method (line 35) gracefully terminates the plugin process.\n\n**gRPC Client Connection to Backend Plugins:**\nThe `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/grpcplugin/grpc_plugin.go` file implements the `grpcPlugin` struct which manages the gRPC connection to backend plugin processes. The `Start()` method (line 68) creates a new plugin client using the `clientFactory` and establishes a gRPC connection. The client configuration is set up in `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/grpcplugin/client.go` via the `newClientConfig()` function (line 61), which configures the hashicorp go-plugin framework with gRPC protocol settings and handshake configuration. The `grpcPlugin` maintains the client state through the `pluginClient` field (line 21) which is a `ClientV2` instance. The `getPluginClient()` method (line 148) retrieves the connected client and checks if the plugin process has exited.\n\n**Health Check Implementation:**\nHealth checks are implemented at multiple layers. The `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/grpcplugin/client_v2.go` file contains the `ClientV2` struct with a `CheckHealth()` method (line 142) that calls the gRPC `DiagnosticsClient.CheckHealth()` method with proper error handling for unimplemented or unavailable services. The `grpcPlugin.CheckHealth()` method (line 183 in grpc_plugin.go) acts as a wrapper that first retrieves a valid client via `getPluginClient()` then delegates to the `ClientV2.CheckHealth()` method. At the higher level, `pkg/plugins/manager/client/client.go` provides the `Service.CheckHealth()` method (line 186) which retrieves the plugin from the registry and calls its health check. The core plugin implementation in `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/coreplugin/core_plugin.go` also implements `CheckHealth()` (line 83) for built-in plugins.\n\n**Plugin Lifecycle Management:**\nThe plugin initialization pipeline in `pkg/plugins/manager/pipeline/initialization/steps.go` defines the `BackendClientStarter` (line 65) which uses the process manager to start backend plugins. The `BackendProcessStartStep()` function (line 71) returns the starter's `Start()` method (line 83) as part of the initialization pipeline. Similarly, `pkg/plugins/manager/pipeline/termination/steps.go` provides `BackendProcessTerminator` (line 15) which calls the process manager's `Stop()` method (line 34) during plugin shutdown.\n\n**Plugin Model:**\nThe main `Plugin` struct in `pkg/plugins/plugins.go` (line 31) acts as a wrapper around the backend client. It delegates `Start()` (line 270), `Stop()` (line 281), `Exited()` (line 316), `CheckHealth()` (line 357), and other operations to the underlying `backendplugin.Plugin` client implementation. This provides a unified interface for all plugin types while allowing different implementations (gRPC-based external plugins, core in-process plugins, etc.).\n\n**Supporting Infrastructure:**\n- `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/ifaces.go` defines the `Plugin` interface that all backend plugins must implement\n- `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/provider/provider.go` provides the `BackendFactory` for creating appropriate plugin implementations\n- `pkg/plugins/manager/process/ifaces.go` defines the `Manager` interface for starting/stopping plugins\n- `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/grpcplugin/client_proto.go` provides protocol buffer client support\n- `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/grpcplugin/log_wrapper.go` wraps logs for gRPC compatibility\n"
}

conversation_events.text:
# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

Use `sg_list_repos` to discover available repositories before searching.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

# Grafana Backend Plugin Management and Lifecycle

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in grafana/grafana that implement backend plugin management: the plugin process supervisor, the gRPC client connection to backend plugins, the plugin health check, and the plugin restart policy on crash.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the platform domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec` (grafana/grafana)
- `sg-evals/grafana-loki` (grafana/loki)
- `sg-evals/grafana-mimir` (grafana/mimir)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find all the Go source files related to backend plugin management in grafana/grafana. Let me start by exploring the repository structure and searching for the relevant components.
Now let me create a comprehensive summary document with all the findings:
Perfect! I've completed a comprehensive analysis of the Grafana backend plugin management system and created the answer file. Let me verify the output:

Excellent! I've completed a comprehensive analysis of the Grafana backend plugin management system. The file `/workspace/answer.json` has been created with detailed findings about all the Go source files involved in:

## Summary of Findings

I identified **15 key files** organized across 4 main areas:

### 1. **Process Supervisor & Restart Policy on Crash**
- `pkg/plugins/manager/process/process.go` - The `Service` struct with `keepPluginAlive()` function that continuously monitors plugin processes and automatically restarts them on crash using a ticker-based loop
- `pkg/plugins/manager/process/ifaces.go` - The `Manager` interface defining Start/Stop contract

### 2. **gRPC Client Connection to Backend Plugins**
- `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/grpcplugin/grpc_plugin.go` - Core gRPC plugin implementation managing client lifecycle
- `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/grpcplugin/client_v2.go` - gRPC V2 client with all plugin methods
- `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/grpcplugin/client.go` - Client configuration using hashicorp go-plugin framework
- `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/grpcplugin/client_proto.go` - Protocol buffer support
- `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/grpcplugin/log_wrapper.go` - Logging integration for gRPC

### 3. **Health Check Implementation**
Multiple layers implementing health checks:
- `ClientV2.CheckHealth()` - Low-level gRPC diagnostics client call
- `grpcPlugin.CheckHealth()` - Middleware checking client availability
- `pkg/plugins/manager/client/client.go` Service - Higher-level plugin registry integration
- `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/coreplugin/core_plugin.go` - Built-in plugin health checks

### 4. **Plugin Lifecycle & Management**
- `pkg/plugins/manager/pipeline/initialization/steps.go` - `BackendClientStarter` initiating plugin processes
- `pkg/plugins/manager/pipeline/termination/steps.go` - `BackendProcessTerminator` shutting down plugins
- `pkg/plugins/plugins.go` - Main `Plugin` struct delegating all operations to backend implementations
- `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/provider/provider.go` - Backend factory provider pattern

The analysis includes **33 key symbols** and a detailed **call chain** showing how these components interact during plugin startup, health checking, and shutdown.

If the plugin exits (`p.Exited()` returns true, line 77), it automatically restarts the plugin by calling `p.Start()` with a `context.Background()` (line 82). This restart policy ensures plugins are automatically recovered from crashes. The `Stop()` method (line 35) gracefully terminates the plugin process.\n\n**gRPC Client Connection to Backend Plugins:**\nThe `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/grpcplugin/grpc_plugin.go` file implements the `grpcPlugin` struct which manages the gRPC connection to backend plugin processes. The `Start()` method (line 68) creates a new plugin client using the `clientFactory` and establishes a gRPC connection. The client configuration is set up in `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/grpcplugin/client.go` via the `newClientConfig()` function (line 61), which configures the hashicorp go-plugin framework with gRPC protocol settings and handshake configuration. The `grpcPlugin` maintains the client state through the `pluginClient` field (line 21) which is a `ClientV2` instance. The `getPluginClient()` method (line 148) retrieves the connected client and checks if the plugin process has exited.\n\n**Health Check Implementation:**\nHealth checks are implemented at multiple layers. The `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/grpcplugin/client_v2.go` file contains the `ClientV2` struct with a `CheckHealth()` method (line 142) that calls the gRPC `DiagnosticsClient.CheckHealth()` method with proper error handling for unimplemented or unavailable services. The `grpcPlugin.CheckHealth()` method (line 183 in grpc_plugin.go) acts as a wrapper that first retrieves a valid client via `getPluginClient()` then delegates to the `ClientV2.CheckHealth()` method. At the higher level, `pkg/plugins/manager/client/client.go` provides the `Service.CheckHealth()` method (line 186) which retrieves the plugin from the registry and calls its health check. The core plugin implementation in `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/coreplugin/core_plugin.go` also implements `CheckHealth()` (line 83) for built-in plugins.\n\n**Plugin Lifecycle Management:**\nThe plugin initialization pipeline in `pkg/plugins/manager/pipeline/initialization/steps.go` defines the `BackendClientStarter` (line 65) which uses the process manager to start backend plugins. The `BackendProcessStartStep()` function (line 71) returns the starter's `Start()` method (line 83) as part of the initialization pipeline. Similarly, `pkg/plugins/manager/pipeline/termination/steps.go` provides `BackendProcessTerminator` (line 15) which calls the process manager's `Stop()` method (line 34) during plugin shutdown.\n\n**Plugin Model:**\nThe main `Plugin` struct in `pkg/plugins/plugins.go` (line 31) acts as a wrapper around the backend client. It delegates `Start()` (line 270), `Stop()` (line 281), `Exited()` (line 316), `CheckHealth()` (line 357), and other operations to the underlying `backendplugin.Plugin` client implementation. This provides a unified interface for all plugin types while allowing different implementations (gRPC-based external plugins, core in-process plugins, etc.).\n\n**Supporting Infrastructure:**\n- `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/ifaces.go` defines the `Plugin` interface that all backend plugins must implement\n- `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/provider/provider.go` provides the `BackendFactory` for creating appropriate plugin implementations\n- `pkg/plugins/manager/process/ifaces.go` defines the `Manager` interface for starting/stopping plugins\n- `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/grpcplugin/client_proto.go` provides protocol buffer client support\n- `pkg/plugins/backendplugin/grpcplugin/log_wrapper.go` wraps logs for gRPC compatibility\n"
}
